notes.completion_rate: 83% completion is solid for a 64-item sprint. 11 items moved forward.
notes.oos_total: 11 OOS additions indicate mid-sprint scope changes. Review planning accuracy and stakeholder alignment.
notes.rollover: 11 items (17%) rolled over from earlier Q sprints. Investigate blockers or sizing issues.
notes.cycle_time: 18d median cycle time is a red flag. Target under 7d. Identify bottlenecks in workflow.
notes.type_split: 39% bugs is borderline high. Monitor quality trends and consider root cause analysis if pattern continues.

notes.completion_rate: 90% completion is solid execution. Sprint closed with 45 of 50 items delivered.
notes.oos_total: 5 OOS items added mid-sprint indicates good scope discipline with minimal reactive work.
notes.rollover: 27 items rolled over (54%) is concerningly high—investigate sprint planning accuracy and capacity estimates for next quarter.
notes.cycle_time: 19d median cycle time is excessive and blocks throughput. Break down work into smaller units and identify bottlenecks causing multi-week delays.
notes.type_split: 40% bugs at upper acceptable threshold. Monitor defect trends—sustained bug volume may indicate tech debt or quality issues upstream.

notes.completion_rate: 78% completion is below target. 13 items incomplete suggests scope was too large or impediments slowed delivery.
notes.oos_total: 5 OOS items added mid-sprint. Low disruption indicates solid planning discipline.
notes.rollover: 24 rollover items (41% of sprint) is high. Review estimation accuracy and identify blockers causing multi-sprint carryover.
notes.cycle_time: 17d median cycle time is concerning—more than double healthy range. Investigate bottlenecks in review/testing or work breakdown size.
notes.type_split: 41% bugs indicates quality debt or insufficient testing upstream. Consider root cause analysis and preventive measures.

notes.completion_rate: 61% completion is below healthy threshold. 23 items incomplete despite sprint close suggests scope overload or estimation issues.
notes.oos_total: Only 3 items added mid-sprint shows strong planning discipline.
notes.rollover: 32 items (54%) carried from previous quarter sprint. High rollover indicates chronic overcommitment or blocked work.
notes.cycle_time: 16d median cycle time is concerning—more than double healthy range. Investigate bottlenecks in workflow.
notes.type_split: 32% bugs is within acceptable range, though near upper threshold. Monitor for upward trend.

notes.completion_rate: Mid-sprint at 33% complete — tracking below target pace for healthy 80%+ closure. Monitor velocity closely through second half.
notes.oos_total: Excellent sprint discipline with only 1 out-of-scope addition. Planning process holding strong.
notes.rollover: Critical: 26 items rolled over (51% of sprint) from prior Q1 sprints. High carryover indicates scope/capacity mismatch or blocked work requiring triage.
notes.cycle_time: 16d median cycle time is concerning — more than double healthy threshold. Investigate blockers, handoffs, or work breakdown size.
notes.type_split: Healthy type distribution at 20% bugs, 39% stories, 41% tasks. Balance supports feature delivery with maintenance work.

notes.completion_rate: 10% complete mid-sprint (5 of 48). Trajectory is weak—team needs to accelerate velocity or descope to hit 60%+ by sprint end.
notes.oos_total: 2 items added post-start. Low unplanned work indicates good sprint discipline.
notes.rollover: 18 items rolled over (38% of sprint). High carryover suggests chronic overcommitment or scope creep—review capacity planning.
notes.cycle_time: 9d median cycle time is elevated. Items are lingering in progress—investigate blockers or break work into smaller chunks.
notes.type_split: 23% bugs, 33% stories, 44% tasks. Healthy distribution, but watch bug influx as sprint progresses.

notes.total: 161 tickets in scope with 21 days remaining. Total is reasonable for a 6-sprint quarter; scope appears stable with 12% out-of-sprint additions indicating disciplined planning.
notes.completed: 98 of 161 tickets completed with 20 days remaining in the quarter. At current pace (1.38 tickets/day), the team is on track to finish the quarter with approximately 126 completed tickets, missing the full scope by ~35 tickets unless velocity increases.
notes.completion_rate: 61% completion rate is below target at this stage. With 78% of the quarter elapsed, completion should be closer to 70-75% to finish strong. The gap signals potential scope overcommitment or capacity constraints.
notes.releases_shipped: 20 releases shipped maintains a healthy 4 per sprint cadence typical for a web platform team. Last release on June 9th confirms continuous delivery momentum.
notes.oos_total: 20 out-of-sprint tickets (12% of total) is within acceptable bounds and indicates reasonably disciplined sprint planning. Two items remain open: DLK-5835 (CSV export, awaiting review) and DLK-5748 (pod state leak, in test).
notes.oos_open: 2 open OOS items remain: DLK-5835 (Farm export enhancement, assigned to Jovan) and DLK-5748 (pod state leak in test with Gianni) — both need resolution before quarter close.
notes.type_split: Bug ratio at 35% (57 of 161) is within acceptable range, though trending high in early sprints (47% in Sprint 95). Recent sprints show healthier mix with bugs dropping to 22-23%, indicating improving quality or better triage discipline.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 4 releases per sprint is healthy for a web platform team. Sprint 98 dropped to 1 release (45% completion) which correlates with the lowest sprint performance of the quarter.
notes.time_logged: 1,728 hours logged against 2,336 estimated represents 74% of planned capacity. Team is under-logging by 608 hours, which could indicate unrealistic estimates, incomplete time tracking, or actual under-utilisation requiring investigation.
notes.estimate_accuracy: Estimates track well at 74% accuracy overall (-26% variance indicates under-logging rather than over-estimation). Sprint-level accuracy was excellent in Sprints 94-97 (94-100%), dropped to 83% in Sprint 98, and shows only 29% in active Sprint 99 — expected mid-sprint as work progresses.
notes.no_estimate: 15 tickets (9%) lack estimates — good hygiene overall. Maintain this discipline to preserve planning reliability and capacity forecasting accuracy.
notes.rollover: 45 tickets (28%) have rolled over across sprints, indicating persistent challenges with sprint predictability. Rollover percentage increased from 19% in Sprint 94 to 38% in the current sprint, suggesting worsening planning accuracy or growing technical complexity.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 16 days is concerning — tickets take over two weeks to complete on average. Mean of 23 days reveals several long-running items dragging overall flow. Target should be under 7 days for a healthy web platform team.
notes.assignee_workload: Sandor Kocsis carries 29% of all tickets (46 total, 35 completed at 76% rate) — workload is concentrated but he's delivering consistently. Dejan Kosanovic (17 tickets, only 35% completion) and Ivor Didovic (16 tickets, 43% completion) are blocked or struggling; intervention needed to unblock them.
